items.title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano sends fiery lava 400 metres into the air
items.summary_auto: The latest lava fountaining episode of an erupting Hawaii volcano shot 400 metres high Tuesday, prompting temporary closures at a national park and part of an important highway because of falling volcanic fragments, including ash.

items.title: The National | Gunshots fired at U.S. consulate in Toronto
items.summary_auto: March 10, 2026 | Police investigate a shooting at the U.S. consulate in Toronto as a ‘national security incident.’ A CBC News investigation reveals a Canadian family helped fuel Myanmar's civil war with pearls. And a fired Coast Guard captain denies he willfully ignored a sinking boat.

items.title: Woman charged with attemped murder after weekend shooting at Rihanna's L.A. home
items.summary_auto: A Florida woman accused of firing gunshots at Rihanna's home in Los Angeles was charged Tuesday with one count of attempted murder and other felony offences, including 10 counts of assault on a person with a semi-automatic firearm.

items.title: Freezing rain storm headed for Ont., Que., Atlantic provinces | Hanomansing Tonight
items.summary_auto: A major storm is expected to sweep through parts of Eastern Canada, potentially causing power outages and dangerous road conditions.

items.title: How high will the U.S.-Israel war with Iran push gas prices? | About That
items.summary_auto: Oil and gas prices are rapidly rising around the world amid the U.S.-Israel war with Iran. Andrew Chang explains what's driving the surge and why predicting the next moves in the oil market is so difficult. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: Australia extends asylum to 2 more Iranian women's soccer team members
items.summary_auto: Two more members of the Iranian women's soccer team were granted asylum in Australia before their teammates departed, the country's Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said Wednesday.

items.title: Early detection prevented worse outcomes in Calgary E. coli outbreak at 11 daycares: study
items.summary_auto: A standardized public health response to an E. coli outbreak at several Calgary daycare centres three years ago resulted in fewer children developing serious health complications, a new study suggests.

items.title: Early detection after E. coli outbreak at Calgary daycares prevented worse outcomes: study
items.summary_auto: A standardized public health response to an E. coli outbreak at several Calgary daycare centres three years ago resulted in fewer children developing serious health complications, a new study suggests.

items.title: Paralympic viewing guide: Mark Arendz eyes 14th career medal
items.summary_auto: CBC Sports' daily newsletter recaps Day 4 of the Paralympic Winter Games and looks at the top Canadians to watch on Wednesday.

items.title: Junior hockey game racks up 568 penalty minutes
items.summary_auto: St. John's Junior Hockey League is promising "severe" suspensions for players after a game devolved into fighting. Players racked up 568 penalty minutes, far surpassing the NHL record of 419 minutes. That record was set in 2004.

items.title: Former CBC News host Travis Dhanraj says he was 'pulled off the air' for fighting against bias
items.summary_auto: Former CBC News host Travis Dhanraj accused the public broadcaster of trying to silence him when he challenged bias at the network and fought for diversity of opinion.

items.title: Regina man imprisoned for crystal meth-fuelled crime spree that ended in fatal collision
items.summary_auto: Mihail Oblacov's life hit "rock bottom essentially right out of the gate" after he was introduced to crystal meth, his lawyer says. His actions while he was in the throes of his addiction in 2024 caused the death of a stranger.

items.title: Up to 16 Liberals considering support for NDP bill to stop Canadian arms flowing to other countries from U.S.
Up to 16 Liberals considering support for NDP's arms-control legislation
items.summary_auto: When the NDP's arms-control bill that seeks to end largely permit-free Canadian military exports to the U.S. is put to a vote on Wednesday, it could garner the support of more than a dozen Liberal MPs. That would be the first time some in caucus split from the government's position on legislation under Prime Minister Mark Carney.

items.title: Canada raises alarm about escalating violence in Lebanon
items.summary_auto: The Canadian government said Tuesday it is alarmed by the escalation of violence and attacks in Lebanon, as the war in the Middle East expands. It called on all sides to uphold humanitarian law.

items.title: Vancouver Coun. Sean Orr sues Mayor Ken Sim for defamation
items.summary_auto: Vancouver Coun. Sean Orr is suing Mayor Ken Sim for allegedly defamatory comments Sim made at an event for members of Chinese-language media in February.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | First Nations leaders unveil items repatriated from Vatican
items.summary_auto: First Nations leaders unveiled five items repatriated to their communities after being at the Vatican for more than a century, during a ceremony at the Canadian Museum of History in Gatineau, Que., on Tuesday.

items.title: Ottawa spending $229M to help tariff-hit Ontario workers obtain new skills
items.summary_auto: The federal government will spend $228.8 million over the next three years to help Ontario workers in industries hit hard by U.S. tariffs acquire new skills and adapt to the trade war disruption.

items.title: Wiarton Willie, Ontario's beloved weather-prognosticating groundhog, is dead
items.summary_auto: Wiarton Willie has died three years after becoming the latest woodchuck to carry on the Groundhog Day tradition begun in 1956 in the Ontario community. "Willie was a curious and charismatic groundhog who delighted both residents and visitors," the Town of South Bruce Peninsula said in announcing the sad news Tuesday.

items.title: Citizen-led recall bid against Alberta premier falls far short of threshold
items.summary_auto: A petition looking to oust Alberta Premier Danielle Smith from her seat in the legislature has failed.

items.title: Heated Rivalry stars Hudson Williams and Francois Arnaud denounce hateful comments
items.summary_auto: Actors Hudson Williams and Francois Arnaud posted matching statements to their Instagram accounts on Monday night, calling out hateful comments made by fans of the hit hockey romance show.

items.title: Canadian Heated Rivalry stars denounce hateful comments
items.summary_auto: Actors Hudson Williams and Francois Arnaud posted matching statements to their Instagram accounts on Monday night, calling out hateful comments made by fans of the hit hockey romance show.

items.title: WNBA, players' union to meet as deadline nears to start season on time: report
items.summary_auto: The WNBA and its players' union met at a hotel Tuesday to continue discussions on reaching a new collective bargaining agreement, with a deadline to get the season started on time at hand.

items.title: Hamilton's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander ties NBA 20-point streak record
items.summary_auto: On Monday night, Canada's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander matched Wilt Chamberlain's NBA record of 126 consecutive regular-season games of 20 or more points.

items.title: Montrealer charged in Ryan Wedding-linked murder plot pleads not guilty
items.summary_auto: A Montreal man charged in connection with a murder plot allegedly ordered by accused drug kingpin Ryan Wedding — and said to involve a Quebec hitman known as Tupac — has pleaded not guilty in a U.S. court.

items.title: Ford says Ontario government will take over Toronto's Billy Bishop airport
items.summary_auto: The province will be taking over control of Billy Bishop airport on Toronto Island, Ontario Premier Doug Ford told reporters at a news conference on Tuesday.

items.title: Ontario family reunited with war medal missing since veteran died in 2011
items.summary_auto: The family of Canadian soldier Stefan Jankowski has been reunited with a military medal that had been missing since the time of his death 2011. He’s from Windsor, Ont., and was a trooper with the Royal Canadian Dragoons, serving in Afghanistan, where he earned the medal.

items.title: Accuser testifies rapper K'naan went from 'charming' to 'monster' at sexual assault trial
items.summary_auto: The sexual assault trial of rapper and filmmaker K'naan began Tuesday at the Quebec City courthouse. He's accused of assaulting a woman in her 20s at the Hilton Quebec hotel in 2010, where he was staying while performing at the Festival d'été de Québec.

items.title: Defence calls more witnesses as billionaire Frank Stronach's sexual assault trial continues
items.summary_auto: The defence began presenting its case Monday, calling several witnesses, including the businessman's former driver. The trial continues at a Toronto courthouse Tuesday.

items.title: 'We got it wrong': N.S. government reverses course on some budget cuts after outcry
items.summary_auto: Supports and programming for people with disabilities, seniors and African Nova Scotian and Indigenous students will be maintained, reversing the cuts that were previously announced.

items.title: Markets steady, crude oil prices ease on Tuesday after volatile start to week
items.summary_auto: Trading steadied early Tuesday and crude prices eased after wild swings this week with investors trying to figure out how long the war with Iran will continue.

items.title: Dozens of Haitian civilians killed by police, security company drones in past year, Human Rights Watch says
items.summary_auto: Drones operated by Haitian security forces and private contractors have killed at least 1,243 people and injured 738 others in less than a year, Human Rights Watch said Tuesday.

items.title: Canadian Coast Guard captain fired for 'willfully' ignoring distress call: labour board
Investigators search Epstein's former New Mexico ranch
items.summary_auto: The search follows New Mexico Attorney General ​Raul Torrez's decision last month to reopen the investigation into the late sex offender's alleged criminal activities at the ranch 48 ​kilometres south of the state's capital of Santa Fe.

items.title: Police release video of suspect vehicle fleeing 'national security incident' shooting at U.S. consulate in Toronto
items.summary_auto: Police say they are looking for two male suspects who reportedly stopped outside the consulate in a white vehicle around 4:30 a.m. and fired multiple rounds at the building. Though police have not provided a possible motive, it follows three separate shootings at synagogues in the Greater Toronto Area at a time of war in the Middle East.

items.title: Manitobans denied assisted dying at 5 times national average, federal report shows
items.summary_auto: A Steinbach woman and her family don't understand why she doesn't qualify for MAID in a province that denies access far more than anywhere else in Canada.

items.title: Notorious N.B. serial killer Allan Legere dead at 78
items.summary_auto: Miramichi serial killer Allan Legere, who terrorized the Miramichi for six months in 1989, has died at the age of 78.

items.title: Why Trump can't afford to brush off the Iran war's economic impact
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is trying to downplay the effect of the Iran war on the domestic economy even as American drivers are already feeling the impact on their wallets every time they fill up their cars and trucks.

items.title: Never smoked before? You could still be at risk of lung cancer, experts say
items.summary_auto: Research shows a growing number of non- and never-smokers are getting diagnosed with lung cancer. Risks for never-smokers include poor air quality caused by pollution and wildfire smoke, occupational exposures like those for firefighters and construction workers, as well as cancer-causing gases like radon found in an increasing number of homes.
